hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of National Senior Health and Fitness Day, which is observed on the last Wednesday of May but since today is the 29th, we are focusing on a feel-good musical that features a plus-sized teenage girl who dreams of dancing on a local TV show, Hairspray is a film that celebrates self-expression, acceptance, and fun.
hero.blurb: Hairspray is a fun and upbeat film that combines great music, impressive dancing, and a story that promotes acceptance and self-expression. With its talented cast and catchy songs, Hairspray is a movie that will leave you feeling energized and inspired. The film's themes of perseverance and individuality make it a great choice for anyone looking for a pick-me-up.

released.title: Hamlet 2
released.reason: Released today in 2008, Hamlet 2 is a hilarious and irreverent comedy that spoofs the classic Shakespearean tale. Released today in 2008, this film still stands out for its outrageous humor and over-the-top performances.
released.blurb: Hamlet 2 is a ridiculous and entertaining film that pokes fun at the conventions of high school theater and the challenges of putting on a production. With its witty dialogue and absurd plot twists, Hamlet 2 is a great choice for anyone looking for a lighthearted and humorous movie experience. The film's talented cast and clever writing make it a standout comedy.

awful.title: The Last Airbender
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, The Last Airbender is often cited as one of the worst film adaptations of all time, and its cringe-worthy acting and confusing plot make it a must-see for fans of so-bad-it's-good cinema.
awful.blurb: The Last Airbender is a mind-bogglingly bad film that fails to deliver on even the most basic levels. With its atrocious acting, incoherent plot, and laughable special effects, The Last Airbender is a movie that will leave you wondering how it ever got made.
